intro: Bubon rigidum, also known as Buckthorn or Rigid Buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, due to its high content of anthraquinone glycosides. The bioactive constituents of Bubon rigidum include anthraquinones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from the plant, such as extracts and teas, are commonly used to support bowel regularity and promote digestive health, although caution is advised due to the plant's potential to cause gastrointestinal irritation.
